title: Repair Grabowski local GitHub DNS resolution path
goal: Restore reliable local GitHub name resolution for Grabowski git and gh operations on heim-pc without weakening resolver, recovery or network-security safeguards.

acceptance.assertion: The failure is reproduced and attributed to the actual local resolver, network namespace or transport boundary; a GitHub outage or general internet failure is not inferred from the original symptom alone.

The repair restores getent or resolvectl resolution for github.com and bounded Grabowski-local gh and git read operations without globally bypassing systemd-resolved or hard-coding GitHub IP addresses as the permanent fix.
The change preserves DNS resolution for unrelated services and records the exact pre-state, changed setting or unit, rollback path and post-state.
After the repair, at least one read-only GitHub API operation and one read-only Git fetch or ls-remote style operation succeed through the same local Grabowski execution path that previously failed.
The healthy GitHub connector remains documented as a fallback transport but is not treated as proof that Grabowski-local DNS is repaired.

metadata.live_finding: Grabowski-local gh calls and git fetch could not resolve github.com. resolvectl query github.com returned REFUSED while direct dig @1.1.1.1 github.com succeeded. systemd-resolved was active. A recovery-gated restart completed successfully but getent/resolvectl resolution still failed. GitHub connector transport remained healthy and was used only as a bounded fallback.
does_not_establish: connector transport equivalence with local git or gh transport
metadata.next_action: Re-read current link and resolver configuration, identify the source of the REFUSED response, apply only a bounded reversible repair, then prove both resolver and local GitHub transport paths before closing.
